sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: RTX division secures $470M Defense Logistics Agency contract
feed.summary: Raytheon, a division of RTX, has secured a significant $470 million contract from the U.S. Defense Logistics Agency. This contract is for the sustainment of the F-135 engine. The work will be performed across various locations, including Oklahoma City; East Hartford, Connecticut; and Indianapolis.

feed.summary: Google's 2026 Discover Core Update is significantly changing how retail investors receive financial news, particularly for Booking Holdings Inc. stock. This proactive information flow helps investors make faster, more informed decisions in the volatile travel sector.

feed.title: Arista Networks (ANET) Valuation Check As 2026 AI Revenue Target Rises To US$3.25b
feed.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) has increased its 2026 AI revenue target to US$3.25 billion, prompting a re-evaluation of its stock. Despite a significant share price increase of 127.07% over the past year, Simply Wall St's narrative suggests Arista Networks is currently 39.2% overvalued with a fair value of $127.06 compared to its last closing price of $176.91. Investors are advised to consider potential competition and overly optimistic AI data center spending expectations.

feed.title: The Amex Platinum Has $3,500 in Annual Value. Here's How to Use Every Dollar.
feed.summary: The American Express Platinum Card, despite its $895 annual fee, offers over $3,500 in annual value through various credits and benefits. This article details how cardholders can maximize each credit, including airline fee credits, hotel credits, Uber Cash, Resy dining credits, digital entertainment, lululemon credits, CLEAR+ membership, Oura Ring credit, and Walmart+ membership, along with extensive airport lounge access. It emphasizes that while the fee is steep, strategic use of these benefits can make the card highly valuable, particularly for frequent travelers and those who align with the card's lifestyle offerings.

feed.title: Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF Rises on Mega-Cap Tech Strength

feed.title: Sirius XM: iHeartMedia Deal Could Be Financially Compelling
feed.summary: Sirius XM Holdings is considered a buy due to improving free cash flow, a strong balance sheet, and a 4.1% dividend yield. While recent M&A talks with iHeartMedia introduce uncertainty, potential cost synergies could make an acquisition financially attractive if iHeartMedia is bought at a sub-6x EV/EBITDA multiple. Sirius XM's standalone outlook projects $1.5 billion in free cash flow by 2027 and at least an 8% annual share count reduction through buybacks.
